info.title: DePix API
info.description:
Public API of DePix — PIX ⇄ DePix (Liquid) payments for merchants, integrators and AI agents.

**Authentication**: Bearer API key (`sk_live_` production / `sk_test_` sandbox) created in the dashboard. Every operation lists its required key scope in `x-required-scope` (`merchant_read`, `merchant_write`, `wallet_read` or `wallet_write`); there is no scope hierarchy. The same endpoints also accept the first-party dashboard JWT.

**Sandbox**: `sk_test_` keys exercise the full surface synthetically — deposits/withdrawals return `sandbox: true` payloads with nothing to pay and create no rows; checkouts/products are created in sandbox mode and completed via the simulate endpoints. Sandbox operations never fire deposit.*/withdraw.* webhooks.

**Conventions**: all amounts are integer cents (BRL); every response carries an `X-Request-Id` header; errors use a dual envelope with a machine-readable `error.code` from a closed catalog; money-moving POSTs accept an optional `Idempotency-Key` header; 429/503 responses carry `Retry-After`. Status polling: 5–15 s intervals are recommended, stopping on the enum values marked `x-terminal: true`.

**Scope of this document**: only the agent/integrator surface. JWT-only dashboard routes (auth/*, merchant create/get + rotate-webhook-secret, api-keys management, reports, uploads, webhook-logs, status, contact, …) are internal SPA contract and intentionally undocumented here. The merchant light-profile PATCH (`PATCH /api/merchants/me`, scope `merchant_write`) is the one merchant route that IS part of the agent surface.

post.description: Generates a PIX QR; when the owner pays it, DePix is delivered to `depixAddress`. Requires scope `wallet_write` on the API-key path. A provider business rejection returns HTTP 400 `validation_error` (the provider message is preserved in `response.errorMessage`) — not a 200. With a sk_test_ key the response is synthetic (`sandbox: true`, no provider call, no row). Track settlement by polling GET /api/deposits/{id} (5–15 s interval) and/or via the deposit.* webhooks. Supports Idempotency-Key.

post.description: Quotes a withdrawal: the response contains the Liquid address to send DePix to. After broadcasting, track via GET /api/withdrawals/{id} and/or withdraw.* webhooks. Requires scope `wallet_write` on the API-key path. **Platform fee (API-key path)**: the response includes `fee_cents` + `fee_address` — the transaction MUST also pay `fee_cents` to `fee_address` as an explicit (unblinded) DePix output in the SAME transaction as the provider output. The fee is verified server-side against the provider-reported settlement transaction; sending the withdrawal without it results in a failed withdrawal and lost funds. sk_test_ returns a synthetic quote (`sandbox: true`, fixed 2% rate, deterministic fee fields, no provider call, no row). Supports Idempotency-Key.

patch.summary: Update the merchant's light profile fields
patch.description: Partial update of the authenticated merchant's LIGHT profile fields (spec §5.6, roadmap decision 14): business_name, website, logo_url, default_callback_url, default_redirect_url. Requires scope `merchant_write` on the API-key path. liquid_address, cnpj and password are owner-only and rejected with 400 validation_error (details.field names the offending field); split_address is never editable here. Read the current profile via GET /api/me. Every PATCH authenticated by an API key emails the account owner naming the changed fields and the key id (compensating control, gate G11).

post.description: Deposit awaiting Pix payment (initial state). Creation does not emit a webhook; this event fires only on a reversal back to this state. Fires ONLY for operations attributed to an API key (human/SPA operations never dispatch) and only when the merchant has a default callback URL configured. Sandbox (sk_test_) operations create no rows, so they never fire webhooks. Outbound POST to the merchant's callback URL, signed with `X-DePix-Signature: t=<unix ts>,v1=<hex hmac-sha256>` where the HMAC is computed over `"${t}.${raw body}"` with the merchant webhook secret. Verify the signature and reject stale timestamps. Delivery is retried on failure: immediate → 1m → 10m → 1h → 4h → 12h (max 6 attempts); use `data.event_id` to deduplicate. Respond with any 2xx quickly to acknowledge.

parameters.description: The delivery's stable event id (evt_*) — same value as `data.event_id`.

event.description: Event name — fixed for this webhook.

Verify the signature and reject stale timestamps. Delivery is retried on failure: immediate → 1m → 10m → 1h → 4h → 12h (max 6 attempts); use `data.event_id` to deduplicate. Respond with any 2xx quickly to acknowledge.

apiKey.description: API key in the Authorization header: `Authorization: Bearer sk_live_…` (production) or `Bearer sk_test_…` (sandbox). Keys are created per merchant in the dashboard with explicit scopes (merchant_read/merchant_write/wallet_read/wallet_write) and, for wallet_write keys, mandatory spending limits. First-party dashboard JWTs are also accepted on the same endpoints.

IdempotencyKey.name: Idempotency-Key
IdempotencyKey.description: Optional idempotency key (1–255 visible ASCII chars). Retrying with the same key and the SAME body replays the stored response (marked with the Idempotency-Replayed: true header) with zero new side effects; the same key with a DIFFERENT body is rejected with 422 idempotency_key_reuse; a concurrent duplicate gets 409 idempotency_in_flight. Keys expire after 24 h. Responses with status 5xx/429 are never stored, so those retries re-execute.

XRequestId.description: Request correlation id — present on EVERY response (success and error). Matches error.request_id in error bodies.

RetryAfter.description: Seconds to wait before retrying (mirrors error.retry_after).

IdempotencyReplayed.description: Present with value "true" when this response was replayed from a stored Idempotency-Key result instead of re-executing the operation.

ErrorEnvelope.description: Dual error envelope (spec §5.1). `response.errorMessage` is the legacy human-readable Portuguese contract; `error` is the additive machine-readable contract for agents (English). Some legacy sibling fields survive for backwards compatibility (`response.errors`, top-level `blocked`).

response.description: Legacy envelope — Portuguese message, first in the body (SPA/integrator contract).

errorMessage.description: Human-readable error message in Portuguese (legacy contract).

errors.description: Per-field validation errors — legacy sibling emitted by POST /api/checkouts on validation_error.

field.description: Name of the offending request field.

message.description: Portuguese validation message for the field.

blocked.description: Legacy sibling present only on 403 `account_blocked` responses (always true when present).

error.description: Machine-readable error object (agent contract, English messages).

code.description: Stable machine-readable error code — the complete closed catalog (ERROR_CATALOG in api/_lib/errors.js). Branch on this, never on messages.

message.description: Human-readable English message for the code.

request_id.description: Request correlation id — also returned on EVERY response (success included) as the X-Request-Id header. Quote it in support requests.

retry_after.description: Seconds to wait before retrying. Present on every 429, 503 and 409 idempotency_in_flight; mirrored in the Retry-After HTTP header.

details.description: Optional machine-readable extras, normative per code: validation_error → {field}; insufficient_scope → {required_scope}; rate_limited → {scope}; amount_out_of_range → {min_cents, max_cents} (bounds of THIS endpoint/mode); account_limit_exceeded → {limit, limit_cents, used_cents?}; key_limit_exceeded → {limit, limit_cents, used_cents}; payer_velocity_limit → {window_minutes, max_per_window}.

required_scope.description: Scope the API key is missing (insufficient_scope).

max_per_window.description: Maximum QRs per payer CPF/CNPJ inside the window (payer_velocity_limit).

used_cents.description: Amount already consumed against the limit, in cents (cumulative/daily limits only).

min_cents.description: Minimum accepted amount in cents for this endpoint/mode.

max_cents.description: Maximum accepted amount in cents for this endpoint/mode.

DepositStatus.description: Deposit lifecycle status (canonical enum from delay-policy.js DEPOSIT_STATUSES). `depix_sent` is the terminal SUCCESS state. Terminal states (safe to stop polling): depix_sent, refunded, canceled, error, expired.

oneOf.description: QR code generated; PIX not paid yet.
PIX paid; payment in pre-settlement review.
PIX paid; waiting for the payer to complete PIX 2FA.
PIX approved by the provider; DePix not yet sent.
Settlement held by the delay policy (new/high-value accounts).
Refund flow started; the deposit will be refunded.
TERMINAL — success: DePix delivered to the target Liquid address.
TERMINAL — deposit refunded to the payer.
TERMINAL — canceled upstream.
TERMINAL — upstream processing error.
TERMINAL — QR expired unpaid.

WithdrawalStatus.description: Withdrawal lifecycle status (canonical enum from delay-policy.js WITHDRAWAL_STATUSES). `sent` is the terminal SUCCESS state. Sandbox status reads return the sandbox-only synthetic value `confirmed` instead (see the sandbox response variant). Terminal states (safe to stop polling): sent, refunded, cancelled, error, expired.

oneOf.description: Created; DePix not yet received by the provider.
DePix received; PIX payout in flight.
TERMINAL — success: PIX delivered to the target key.
TERMINAL — refunded.
TERMINAL — cancelled.
TERMINAL — upstream processing error.
TERMINAL — DePix never arrived (swept by cron).

CheckoutStatus.description: Checkout lifecycle status (canonical enum from checkout.js CHECKOUT_STATUSES, matching the checkouts.status CHECK constraint). `completed` is the terminal SUCCESS state. Terminal states (safe to stop polling): completed, expired, cancelled.

oneOf.description: Awaiting payment; the PIX QR is active until expires_at.
PIX paid; settlement in progress.
Payment approved by the provider; DePix on the way to the merchant.
TERMINAL — success: DePix delivered to the merchant.
TERMINAL — QR expired unpaid.
TERMINAL — cancelled upstream by the provider (refund/error).

DepositCreateRequest.description: Personal deposit request: generates a PIX QR whose payment delivers DePix to `depixAddress`.

amountInCents.description: Deposit amount in cents (BRL). Accepted range: 500–300000 (R$ 5,00–R$ 3.000,00). Account-level and per-key limits may restrict it further.

depixAddress.description: Liquid address that will receive the DePix once the PIX settles.

payer_tax_number.description: CPF or CNPJ of the PIX payer. Required when the platform tax-number gate is on (currently ON in production); always validated and forwarded upstream when present.

DepositCreateResponse.description: Deposit creation result (HTTP 200 — success only). A provider business rejection is NOT a 200: it returns HTTP 400 `validation_error` with the upstream message preserved in `response.errorMessage`, mirroring POST /api/withdraw.

async.description: Always false — QR generation is synchronous.

qrCopyPaste.description: PIX copy-and-paste (BR Code) payload the payer uses. In sandbox this is a SANDBOX-…-DO-NOT-PAY placeholder that must never be paid.

qrImageUrl.description: URL (or data URI) of the rendered QR image. Always set on live; null on sandbox (nothing to render).

id.description: Deposit id — poll GET /api/deposits/{id} with it. Prefixed sandbox_ in test mode.

sandbox.description: Present and true only on sk_test_ responses (synthetic sandbox: no PIX, no DePix, nothing to pay).

DepositStatusResponse.description: Deposit status read (stable EN subset — spec §2.6). Live rows and the fixed synthetic sandbox variant.

oneOf.description: Live deposit row (JWT or sk_live_).

id.description: Deposit id (the id returned by POST /api/deposit).

updated_at.description: Last update timestamp (UTC). Only settlement is individually timestamped today, so this equals created_at until depix_sent.

rejection_reasons.description: Provider reasons a deposit was refused/held — e.g. PAYER_MISMATCH, PAST_DAILY_LIMIT, BLOCKED_USER, HIGH_VELOCITY. Empty array when the deposit was not refused. New reason codes may appear; display unknown codes as-is.

oneOf.description: Sandbox synthetic status (sk_test_ key + sandbox_* id): a fixed, stateless terminal success so agents can exercise the full polling loop in test mode. Echoes amount_cents + deterministic timestamps so the shape matches the live variant.

id.description: The sandbox_* id passed in the path.

amount_cents.description: Deposit amount in cents (BRL), decoded from the sandbox_* id minted at creation — mirrors the live read so agents can confirm the value in test mode. null only for legacy/malformed sandbox ids that carry no embedded amount.

status.description: Fixed synthetic terminal success state (always depix_sent in sandbox).

created_at.description: Deterministic synthetic creation timestamp (UTC, `YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS`) — fixed in sandbox (stateless).

updated_at.description: Deterministic synthetic update timestamp (UTC, `YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS`) — equals created_at in sandbox.

rejection_reasons.description: Always an empty array in sandbox — the synthetic terminal state is a success, so there is never a reason. Present so agents learn the field's shape in test mode.

WithdrawCreateRequest.description: Withdrawal (Liquid→PIX) request. Send EXACTLY ONE of depositAmountInCents ("you send" mode) or payoutAmountInCents ("you receive" mode). A legacy `depixAddress` field is accepted and ignored.

pixKey.description: Destination PIX key (email, phone, CPF/CNPJ or random key).

depositAmountInCents.description: "You send" mode: DePix amount you will deliver, in cents. Range: 500–600000. Mutually exclusive with payoutAmountInCents.

payoutAmountInCents.description: "You receive" mode: PIX amount the destination key receives, in cents. Range: 500–588000 (fee-adjusted). Mutually exclusive with depositAmountInCents.

taxNumber.description: CPF/CNPJ of the PIX key holder. Required when the platform tax-number gate is on (currently ON in production).

WithdrawCreateResponse.description: Withdrawal quote: send DePix to `response.depositAddress` — and, on the API-key path, the platform fee (`fee_cents`) to `fee_address` in the SAME transaction. Track via GET /api/withdrawals/{id} and/or withdraw.* webhooks.

withdrawalId.description: Withdrawal id — use it in GET /api/withdrawals/{id}. Prefixed sandbox_ in test mode.

depositAddress.description: Liquid address to send the DePix to. In sandbox this is a SANDBOX-…-DO-NOT-PAY placeholder — never send funds to it.

depositAmountInCents.description: DePix amount the provider expects to receive, in cents.

payoutAmountInCents.description: PIX amount the destination key will receive, in cents.

totalDepositAmountInCents.description: Gross wallet outflow: provider amount + service fee.

split.description: Duplicate of fee_cents/fee_address in an older shape; read fee_cents/fee_address instead.

address.description: Liquid address of the platform fee output.

amountCentavos.description: Fee amount in cents for the second output.

fee_cents.description: Platform fee in cents (API-key path). MANDATORY: pay this amount to `fee_address` as an explicit (unblinded) DePix output in the SAME transaction as the provider output. The fee is verified server-side against the provider-reported settlement transaction — a transaction without it results in a failed withdrawal and lost funds.

fee_address.description: Non-confidential (ex1) Liquid address for the platform fee output (API-key path). Pay it EXACTLY as given — a confidential/blinded output cannot be verified and counts as an unpaid fee.

sandbox.description: Present and true only on sk_test_ responses (synthetic quote, fixed 2% rate).

WithdrawalStatusResponse.description: Withdrawal status read (stable EN subset — spec §2.6). Live rows and the fixed synthetic sandbox variant.

oneOf.description: Live withdrawal row (JWT or sk_live_).

id.description: Withdrawal id (the withdrawalId returned by POST /api/withdraw).

amount_cents.description: Provider-side deposit amount in cents (what the caller sends) — consistent with the per-key daily accumulator.

updated_at.description: Last update timestamp (UTC). No per-status timestamps exist on withdrawals today, so this equals created_at.

liquid_txid.description: Settlement Liquid txid (the provider-reported on-chain tx that funded the withdrawal) — present once the provider reports it.

oneOf.description: Sandbox synthetic status (sk_test_ key + sandbox_* id): fixed, stateless terminal state for test-mode polling. Echoes amount_cents + deterministic timestamps so the shape matches the live variant.

id.description: The sandbox_* id passed in the path.

amount_cents.description: Provider-side deposit amount in cents (what the caller sends), decoded from the sandbox_* id minted at creation — mirrors the live read. null only for legacy/malformed sandbox ids that carry no embedded amount.

created_at.description: Deterministic synthetic creation timestamp (UTC, `YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS`) — fixed in sandbox (stateless).

updated_at.description: Deterministic synthetic update timestamp (UTC, `YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS`) — equals created_at in sandbox.

description.description: Free-text description shown on the checkout page (max 500 chars).

image_url.description: HTTPS image displayed on the checkout page.

callback_url.description: HTTPS webhook endpoint for this checkout's checkout.* events (overrides the merchant default).

redirect_url.description: HTTPS URL the payer is redirected to after completion.

metadata.description: Arbitrary JSON object echoed back in reads and webhooks (max 4KB serialized).

expires_in.description: QR lifetime in seconds. Range: 300–1200; default 1200.

payer_tax_number.description: CPF/CNPJ of the payer. Required when the platform tax-number gate is on (currently ON in production).

CheckoutCreateResponse.description: Created checkout with its PIX payload and hosted payment page.

description.description: Description echoed back (null when not provided).

image_url.description: Image URL echoed back (null when not provided).

is_live.description: False for sandbox checkouts (sk_test_): PIX payload is a non-payable placeholder.

payment_url.description: Hosted checkout page URL to hand to the payer.

qr_code.description: PIX copy-and-paste (BR Code) payload. SANDBOX-…-DO-NOT-PAY placeholder in test mode.

pix_payload.description: PIX copy-and-paste payload — present only while status is "pending".

rejection_reasons.description: Provider reasons for a refunded/held payment on the underlying deposit — e.g. PAYER_MISMATCH, PAST_DAILY_LIMIT, BLOCKED_USER, HIGH_VELOCITY. Empty array when the payment was not refused. New reason codes may appear; display unknown codes as-is.

callback_url.description: Webhook endpoint receiving this checkout's checkout.* events.

metadata.description: Merchant metadata. Parsed back to an object when valid JSON; the raw string is preserved otherwise.

processing_at.description: When the checkout entered processing (null if never).

approved_at.description: When the checkout was approved (null if never).

completed_at.description: When the checkout completed (null if never).

cancelled_at.description: When the checkout was cancelled (null if never).

blockchain_tx_id.description: Liquid transaction id of the DePix settlement (null until completed).

CheckoutGetResponse.description: Envelope of the authenticated checkout read.

CheckoutListResponse.description: Paginated checkout list with aggregate stats.

processing_at.description: When the checkout entered processing (null if never).

approved_at.description: When the checkout was approved (null if never).

metadata.description: Merchant metadata as the raw stored JSON string (not parsed on the list endpoint).

product_name.description: Name of the linked product (null for generic checkouts).

rejection_reasons.description: Provider reasons for a refunded/held payment on the underlying deposit — e.g. PAYER_MISMATCH, PAST_DAILY_LIMIT, BLOCKED_USER, HIGH_VELOCITY. Empty array when the payment was not refused. New reason codes may appear; display unknown codes as-is.

stats.description: Aggregates over the CURRENT FILTER (not just the current page).

completed_amount.description: Sum of completed amounts in cents.

MeResponse.description: Identity of the authenticated merchant — probe endpoint for key validation.

merchant_slug.description: Public URL slug of the merchant page (pay.depixapp.com/{merchant_slug}).

is_live.description: False when authenticated with a sk_test_ key (sandbox mode).

MerchantUpdateRequest.description: Partial merchant-profile update — only the provided fields change (spec §5.6). On the API-key path ONLY these 5 light fields are editable; sending liquid_address, cnpj or password is rejected with 400 validation_error (owner + web dashboard only). split_address is never editable here (admin only). Every key-authenticated PATCH emails the account owner (compensating control).

business_name.description: New business name (2–100 chars). Changing it rotates the public merchant_slug and retires the old one, so any outstanding payment links or checkout URLs built on the old slug will 404 — rename with that in mind.

website.description: New website URL (normalized to https://; null/empty clears it).

logo_url.description: New HTTPS logo URL (null/empty clears it).

default_callback_url.description: New HTTPS default webhook endpoint for deposit.*/withdraw.* events (null/empty clears it).

default_redirect_url.description: New HTTPS default post-payment redirect for this merchant's customers (null/empty clears it).

merchant_slug.description: The merchant's public URL slug after the update (unchanged unless business_name changed).

ProductCreateRequest.description: Product creation request (reusable checkout template with a public page).

slug.description: URL slug (lowercase letters, digits, hyphens). Auto-generated from name when omitted.

callback_url.description: HTTPS webhook endpoint for checkouts created from this product.

redirect_url.description: HTTPS post-payment redirect for checkouts created from this product.

metadata.description: Arbitrary JSON copied into each checkout (max 4KB serialized).

expires_in.description: Checkout QR lifetime in seconds. Range: 300–1200; default 1200.

slug.description: URL slug (unique per merchant and mode).

image_url.description: Product image URL (null when not provided).

callback_url.description: Webhook endpoint for this product's checkouts (null when not provided).

metadata.description: Metadata echoed back (null when not provided).

ProductListItem.description: Product list row with checkout aggregates.

position.description: Pinned position on the public page (null = not pinned).

total_checkouts.description: Total checkouts created from this product.

completed_amount.description: Sum of completed amounts in cents.

ProductGetResponse.description: Single product with checkout aggregates. NOTE: `product` mirrors the full database row (SELECT *), so additional columns may appear as the schema evolves — additionalProperties is intentionally open here.

callback_url.description: Webhook endpoint for this product's checkouts.

metadata.description: Metadata (parsed to an object when valid JSON).

position.description: Pinned position on the public page (null = not pinned).

total.description: Total checkouts created from this product.

completed_amount.description: Sum of completed amounts in cents.

ProductUpdateRequest.description: Partial product update — only the provided fields change. Nullable fields (description, image_url, callback_url, redirect_url, metadata) accept null/empty string to clear.

slug.description: New URL slug (must stay unique per merchant and mode).

image_url.description: New HTTPS image URL (null/empty clears it).

callback_url.description: New HTTPS webhook endpoint (null/empty clears it).

metadata.description: New metadata object (null clears it, max 4KB serialized).

expires_in.description: New checkout QR lifetime in seconds (300–1200).

ProductCheckoutsResponse.description: Paginated checkouts of one product, with aggregates.

checkouts.description: Checkout rows for the product, newest first.

processing_at.description: When the checkout entered processing (null if never).

completed_at.description: When the checkout completed (null if never).

completed_amount.description: Sum of completed amounts in cents.

ProductPublicResponse.description: Public product data (no auth) — powers the public product page.

username.description: Legacy alias of merchant_slug (kept for integrators; read merchant_slug instead).

is_test.description: Always false here — only live products are served publicly.

PublicProductCheckoutResponse.description: Checkout created from a public product page.

amount.description: Charge amount in cents (the product price).

description.description: Product description carried onto the checkout.

is_live.description: Always true — only live products accept public checkouts.

PayCheckoutResponse.description: Public checkout data (no auth) — poll it to track payment from the payer side.

merchant_username.description: Legacy alias of merchant_slug (kept for integrators; read merchant_slug instead).

remaining_seconds.description: Seconds until QR expiry (0 when expired or not pending).

is_test.description: True for sandbox checkouts (non-payable placeholder QR).

pix.description: PIX payment payload — present only while status is "pending".

FeaturedResponse.description: Result of reconciling the pinned product set.

featured.description: The pinned product ids in their new display order.

HealthResponse.description: Dependency health. The HTTP status is the contract: 200 = healthy, 503 = degraded.

status.description: "ok" when every dependency check passed, "degraded" otherwise (mirrors the HTTP status).

DepositWebhookData.description: Deposit event payload — same EN shape as GET /api/deposits/{id}, plus the delivery id.

id.description: Deposit id (the id returned by POST /api/deposit).

status.description: The status the deposit just transitioned to (matches the event name).

rejection_reasons.description: Provider reasons a deposit was refused/held — e.g. PAYER_MISMATCH, PAST_DAILY_LIMIT, BLOCKED_USER, HIGH_VELOCITY. Empty array when the deposit was not refused. New reason codes may appear; display unknown codes as-is.

event_id.description: Stable delivery id (evt_*) — identical across retries of the same event; use it for deduplication. Also sent as the X-DePix-Event-Id header.

WithdrawalWebhookData.description: Withdrawal event payload — same EN shape as GET /api/withdrawals/{id}, plus the delivery id.

id.description: Withdrawal id (the withdrawalId returned by POST /api/withdraw).

status.description: The status the withdrawal just transitioned to (matches the event name).

liquid_txid.description: Settlement Liquid txid (the provider-reported on-chain tx that funded the withdrawal) — present once the provider reports it.

event_id.description: Stable delivery id (evt_*) — identical across retries of the same event; use it for deduplication. Also sent as the X-DePix-Event-Id header.

status.description: The status the checkout just transitioned to (matches the event name).

metadata.description: Merchant metadata when set on the checkout (omitted otherwise).

blockchain_tx_id.description: Liquid txid of the settlement — present on checkout.completed when known.

event_id.description: Stable delivery id (evt_*) — identical across retries of the same event; use it for deduplication. Also sent as the X-DePix-Event-Id header.
